message:
feat(messaging): add OperatorWorkloadShape for explicit service class selection

Introduces OperatorWorkloadShape enum allowing operators to declare
a semantic workload shape when the generic Interactive/DurablePipeline
inference is insufficient:

  - General (default): let the compiler infer from other intent fields
  - ReadModel: read-side materializations and derived views
  - LowValueFanout: wide fanout where partial degradation is
    preferable to stronger delivery contracts
  - ExpensiveReplay: replay-heavy or forensic work that is valuable
    but expensive to keep hot

The compile_service_class function now checks workload_shape before
falling back to the existing cross-tenant/sovereignty/egress
heuristics, so explicit operator declarations take precedence.

New field on OperatorIntent:
  - workload_shape: OperatorWorkloadShape (serde default = General)
  - with_workload_shape() builder method

New SemanticServiceClass variants mapped:
  - ReadModel → ReadModel
  - LowValueFanout → LowValueFanout (with ReduceFanout degradation)
  - ExpensiveReplay → ExpensiveReplay (with PauseReplay degradation,
    ForensicReplayable delivery)

Three new tests:
  - compiler_maps_read_model_workload_shape
  - compiler_maps_low_value_fanout_workload_shape
  - compiler_maps_expensive_replay_to_forensic_lane

Re-export added to messaging/mod.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
